text: The surgeon administers 5 ml of indocyanine green via a peripheral cannula. A near-infrared laparoscope is used through a proctoscope to visualize the colorectal anastomosis internally, while a second laparoscope provides an external view from the abdominal side.

The video shows excellent perfusion of the colorectal anastomosis, with fluorescence visible both proximal and distal to the circular stapled anastomosis. The serosal aspect of the bowel also demonstrates excellent perfusion, as observed through the second laparoscopic system.
